_doc: Per-asset-class size budgets enforced by tools/audits/budgets.py. A failing budget blocks merge to main (see docs/architecture.md). Sizes are uncompressed bytes unless suffixed with .gz.

_transition_status.v7.0-dev: Three budgets are tracked as 'WARN' until the corresponding todos land: landing-html (waits on ssg-per-uc-category — slim landing page replaces the SPA shell), fingerprinted-css and fingerprinted-js (wait on catalog-index-lazy + search-shards which split the bundles, plus a future minification pass). The transitional bundle is the entire legacy inline CSS/JS extracted verbatim — uncompressed and unsplit by design so the v7.0 cutover preserves byte-for-byte UI behaviour. Violations during transition are expected; CI logs them but does not block.

budgets.rationale: Landing page must render fast on the first paint without lazy-loaded JS. Currently the SPA shell; replaced with a slim landing page in the ssg-per-uc-category todo.
Per-UC page is a leaf article, mostly text + JSON-LD. Strict cap protects long-tail UCs.

Category pages list per-category UCs as a static index. Largest categories (cat-10 with ~2.4k UCs) currently exceed the original 120 KiB target. Bumped to 2 MiB raw / 256 KiB gz as a transitional cap; a future ssg-category-pagination todo will paginate large categories (e.g. /category/<slug>/page-2/) and tighten the cap back below 200 KiB raw.
Regulation rollups can be large; same cap as category.
Interactive SPA shell. Heavy by design but must stay under 1 MiB raw.
CSS is critical-path; aggressive cap forces tree-shaking. Current bundle is the entire legacy inline CSS extracted verbatim; splits per-route once render_pages.py SSG lands.

Total JS budget for app + filters + search + panel + theme + sw. Current bundle is the entire legacy inline JS extracted verbatim; lazy-fetched chunks land in catalog-index-lazy and search-shards todos.
The browse-bootstrap index — must stay tiny enough to ship in one TCP window.

Shards are loaded lazily on first keystroke; the gzipped size is the actual network cost since GitHub Pages always negotiates content-encoding. 128 KiB gz covers the largest shard (shard-10 at ~103 KiB gz) with margin and still fits comfortably in the initial congestion window. The raw cap catches an order-of-magnitude regression.

Vocab is fetched once per session on first keystroke and gates every shard fetch. 64 KiB gz keeps it under one TCP segment after slow-start ramp-up; raw cap leaves headroom for the catalogue to ~12k UCs.
Soft cap; oversized UC payloads warrant a human review but do not block merge.
Iframeable cards must stay tiny so embedders don't pay a tax.
Auto-resize + postMessage helper. Tighter than the main app bundle.
